channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Retaliatory strikes between Israel and Iran risk pulling region back into war
item.description: Israel and Iran exchanged fire early Monday, escalating tensions and raising fears the conflict could pull the region back into a full-scale war.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran exchanged fire early Monday, escalating tensions and raising fears the conflict could pull the region back into a full-scale war.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Israel and Iran trade fire, threatening the progress between the U.S. and Tehran
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Randa Slim of the Stimson Center about how the latest round of retaliatory strikes from Iran and Israel could affect the peace talks between the U.S. and Tehran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Randa Slim of the Stimson Center about how the latest round of retaliatory strikes from Iran and Israel could affect the peace talks between the U.S. and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Pope Leo calls for an end to political polarization during visit to Spain
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is in Spain, calling for an end to political polarization on his first papal visit to the country in 15 years.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo XIV is in Spain, calling for an end to political polarization on his first papal visit to the country in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Can a vibrating belt help protect bones and muscle health?
item.description: More than 40 million adults in the U.S. ages 50 and older have osteopenia, or low bone density. An FDA-approved wearable vibration device is giving some women a tool that could slow that loss.
item.content:encoded:
More than 40 million adults in the U.S. ages 50 and older have osteopenia, or low bone density. An FDA-approved wearable vibration device is giving some women a tool that could slow that loss.

(Image credit: Maria Fabrizio for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes, Trump walked out of an interview after being pressed on election fraud claims, ebola outbreak is spreading at alarming rate.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes, Trump walked out of an interview after being pressed on election fraud claims, ebola outbreak is spreading at alarming rate.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 'Schmigadoon!' wins best musical at 79th Annual Tony Awards
item.description: The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway on Sunday. Jeff Lunden breaks down the results of Broadway's biggest night.
item.content:encoded: The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway on Sunday. Jeff Lunden breaks down the results of Broadway's biggest night.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden

item.title: Recently recovered recording features voice of Harpo Marx, the 'silent' Marx brother
item.description: Harpo Marx -- the "silent" Marx brother -- can finally be heard speaking in a live album of recently recovered material, which was recorded just six months before he died in 1964.
item.content:encoded: Harpo Marx -- the "silent" Marx brother -- can finally be heard speaking in a live album of recently recovered material, which was recorded just six months before he died in 1964.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Former Kennedy Center curator talks about the venue's future
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Political scandals once ended careers. Are they as powerful as they once were?
item.description: There was a time when scandals were the death knell for political careers. But today, they're far from being career enders. Do scandals really not hold any power anymore?
item.content:encoded: There was a time when scandals were the death knell for political careers. But today, they're far from being career enders. Do scandals really not hold any power anymore?
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Chinese president visits North Korea for the first time in 7 years
item.description: China's President Xi Jinping is in North Korea, his first trip in seven years, in a bid to reassert China's influence in the region.
item.content:encoded: China's President Xi Jinping is in North Korea, his first trip in seven years, in a bid to reassert China's influence in the region.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn
